title: Basic Authentication in Node.js
body:
When building a small admin panel without external frameworks, Basic Authentication is one of the simplest ways to protect routes. It works by sending a base64-encoded username and password in the Authorization header.

In a Node.js HTTP server, we can access this value using req.headers.authorization. If the header does not exist, it means the browser has not yet provided credentials. In that case, we respond with status 401 and include a WWW-Authenticate header. This tells the browser to display the login popup.

Although Basic Auth is simple and easy to implement, it should only be used over HTTPS in real applications. For learning and internal tools, however, it is a great way to understand how authentication works at the HTTP protocol level.",
